key_insight: Vilya-1 is an all-atom foundation model that can better predict and design macrocycle structures across diverse chemistries, while also estimating developability properties like membrane permeability.
clinical_relevance: Although not a cancer-specific drug discovery system, it could accelerate discovery of macrocycle-based therapeutics by improving structure prediction and early developability screening.
method: Uses a uniform all-atom representation for macrocycles and small molecules.
Trained on heterogeneous structural datasets spanning diverse topologies and chemical classes.
Supports both structure prediction and generative design of novel macrocycles.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports substantially improved geometric accuracy versus physics-based methods, co-folding networks, and deep-learning conformer generators across a broad set of canonical and non-canonical macrocycles.
watch_item: Broad benchmark gains may not directly translate to real-world synthesis, assay performance, or prospective therapeutic success without additional validation.
paper_title: Vilya-1: An all-atom foundation model for macrocycle structure prediction and design
